tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Low total CO₂ may indicate metabolic acidosis. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria) are abnormal and may reflect renal dysfunction and/or metabolic derangement. Low HbA1c and normal serum glucose do not support chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for acute or chronic kidney disease and possible metabolic causes.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
